title: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Alert Volume by Detection Source
dataSources: Microsoft 365 Defender alert feed via `sourcetype=ms:defender:atp:alerts` (Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security)
spl:
index=* sourcetype="ms:defender:atp:alerts" earliest=-24h
| eval src=coalesce(detectionSource, DetectionSource, "unknown")
| eval sev=lower(coalesce(severity, Severity, ""))
| where sev IN ("high","medium")
| stats count by src, category, signature
| sort -count
description: High and medium Defender for Endpoint alerts grouped by detection source and category expose noisy engines, mis-tuned custom indicators, and sudden shifts toward a single detection pipeline that may indicate an active campaign or ingestion drift.
value: Surfaces which Defender detection sources drive alert volume so SOC leads can tune automation, suppressions, and hunting priorities before queues overflow.
implementation: Install Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security and enable the Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Alerts modular input (or equivalent API collection). Send data to a dedicated security index. Confirm field extractions for `detectionSource`, `severity`, `category`, and `signature` match your add-on version; rename in SPL if your deployment uses alternate spellings. Schedule hourly for trending; alert on week-over-week spikes for a single detection source.
visualization: Stacked bar chart (alerts by detection source), treemap (category × source), timechart of total high/medium alerts.

references.title: Source types — Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security

knownFalsePositives: Defender volume by source can swing when we enable a new data connector, a preview feature lights up, or a test machine floods test alerts.
grandmaExplanation: We see which Microsoft defender streams are loudest so we can tune, route, or re-scope detections on that platform without drowning the SOC.
